name: Jira Defect Flow
description: End-to-end orchestration for a Jira defect: pull the ticket, triage, hand off to the `bug-fix` skill for reproduction-first / root-cause / minimum-diff discipline, open a PR with the Jira key in the Closes footer, comment + transition the ticket via the `jira` skill, and optionally run the consumer repo's dev-deploy hook. Pure choreography — composes existing skills rather than re-implementing them.
author: dropkit

skills: jira (in this repo) — for all Jira reads, comments, and transitions
bug-fix (in agent-ready-repo) — for the reproduction / RCA / minimum-fix discipline
adversarial-reviewer, security-reviewer, quality-engineer (in agent-ready-repo) — for the review pass before PR

input.arguments: a Jira issue key (e.g., PROJ-123), optionally with a target environment or deploy hook override

env.DEPLOY_DEV_CMD: Optional. Shell command this skill runs to deploy the merged fix to the dev environment. If unset, the skill stops at PR-merged and asks the user how to deploy. Consumer repos may instead provide an executable `.context/deploy_dev.sh`.
env.JIRA_DEFECT_FIX_PREFIX: Optional. Branch prefix override (default: `fix`). Use e.g. `bugfix` or `hotfix` if your repo convention differs.

artifacts: git branch fix/<key>-<slug> with one or more commits
Jira comments and a transition on <KEY>
